UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents an individual employee's profile within the ITG Connect application. Contains personal details and references to organizational data.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

email.description: The user's email address, typically obtained from the authentication provider. (Relationship: Implicit 1:1 with Auth System)

firstName.description: The first name of the user.

lastName.description: The last name of the user.

profilePhotoUrl.description: URL to the user's profile photo.

birthDate.description: The user's date of birth, used for birthday notifications.

departmentId.description: Reference to the Department this user belongs to. (Relationship: Department 1:N UserProfile)

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile was last updated.

Department.title: Department
Department.description: Represents an organizational department within ITG Connect, allowing users to categorize themselves.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Department entity.

description.description: A brief description of the department's function or responsibilities.

Achievement.title: Achievement
Achievement.description: Represents a specific accomplishment or recognition for an employee, displayed in the centralized feed and user profiles.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Achievement entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who achieved this. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Achievement)

description.description: A detailed description of the achievement.

dateAwarded.description: The date when the achievement was awarded or completed.

category.description: An optional category for the achievement (e.g., 'Professional', 'Teamwork', 'Innovation').

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the achievement record was created.

Wish.title: Wish
Wish.description: Represents a personalized birthday wish sent from one employee to another.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Wish entity.

senderId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who sent the wish. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Wish as Sender)

recipientId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who received the wish. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Wish as Recipient)

message.description: The personalized message of the wish.

sentAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the wish was sent.

NewsPost.title: NewsPost
NewsPost.description: Represents an internal news article or announcement posted by an administrator for the entire organization.

id.description: Unique identifier for the NewsPost entity.

title.description: The title of the news post.

content.description: The main content or body of the news post.

postedByUserId.description: Reference to the UserProfile (administrator) who posted the news. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N NewsPost)

postedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the news post was published.

isArchived.description: Boolean flag indicating if the news post has been archived.

Event.title: Event
Event.description: Represents a company event or activity, posted by an administrator for employees to browse.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Event entity.

title.description: The title or name of the event.

description.description: A detailed description of the event.

location.description: The physical or virtual location where the event will take place.

startTime.description: The start date and time of the event.

endTime.description: The end date and time of the event.

postedByUserId.description: Reference to the UserProfile (administrator) who created the event. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Event)

postedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the event record was created or posted.

rsvpRequired.description: Boolean flag indicating whether an RSVP is required for the event.

definition.description: Stores individual employee profiles. The document ID `{userId}` corresponds to the authenticated user's UID, ensuring path-based ownership. Read access is global for authenticated users to facilitate interaction (e.g., sending wishes), while write access is restricted to the owner matching `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user, matching request.auth.uid.

definition.description: Defines organizational departments. This collection is globally readable by all authenticated users. Write access (create, update, delete) is restricted to administrators, managed through a separate role-based access control system (e.g., an `/roles_admin/{uid}` collection) to maintain authorization independence from this data structure.

params.name: departmentId
params.description: The unique ID of the department.

definition.description: Stores individual achievements for each user. The `{userId}` in the path identifies the owner, and the document includes a denormalized `userId` field (matching the path) for robust authorization independence. Write access is restricted to the owner (`request.auth.uid == userId`), while read access is global for authenticated users to support the organization-wide achievement feed via collection group queries.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who owns the achievement.

params.name: achievementId
params.description: The unique ID of the achievement.

definition.description: Stores personalized birthday wishes received by a specific user. The `{recipientId}` in the path identifies the recipient. The document includes denormalized `senderId` and `recipientId` fields to ensure authorization independence. Create access requires `request.auth.uid` to match the `senderId`. Read/list access is restricted to the recipient (`request.auth.uid == recipientId`). This structure supports QAPs for recipients to list their own wishes securely.

params.name: recipientId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who is the recipient of the wish.

params.name: wishId
params.description: The unique ID of the wish.

definition.description: Contains organization-wide news articles and announcements. This collection is globally readable by all authenticated users. Write access is restricted to administrators, relying on an external role check for authorization independence.

params.name: newsPostId
params.description: The unique ID of the news post.

definition.description: Stores company events and activities. This collection is globally readable by all authenticated users. Write access is restricted to administrators, relying on an external role check for authorization independence.

params.name: eventId
params.description: The unique ID of the event.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure for 'ITG Connect' is designed with a strong emphasis on Authorization Independence, enabling robust and debuggable security rules. The core strategy revolves around denormalizing authorization context and employing structural segregation for different security postures.

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):**
This design prioritizes avoiding hierarchical authorization dependencies that rely on `get()` calls in security rules. 
1.  For user-specific subcollections like `/user_profiles/{userId}/achievements/{achievementId}` and `/user_profiles/{recipientId}/received_wishes/{wishId}`, the relevant authorization fields (`userId`, `senderId`, `recipientId`) are explicitly denormalized into each document. This means rules can directly compare `request.auth.uid` with the document's own fields (e.g., `request.resource.data.userId`) and the path parameters (`userId`, `recipientId`) without needing to fetch parent documents. This allows for atomic write operations and simplifies rule logic considerably.
2.  Global administrative roles are handled by checking a dedicated, flat collection (e.g., `/roles_admin/{uid}`). This `get()` operation for role checking is an acceptable exception as it is not a hierarchical dependency on parent *data* but a global permission check, maintaining atomicity for data operations.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
The structure inherently supports secure `list` operations by ensuring that collection paths and document fields align with authorization requirements:
1.  **Private User Data:** `/user_profiles/{userId}/achievements` allows a user to securely list their own achievements by matching `request.auth.uid` to the path's `userId`. Similarly, `/user_profiles/{recipientId}/received_wishes` enables recipients to list only their own wishes.
2.  **Global Feeds/Shared Data:** Top-level collections like `/news_posts`, `/events`, and `/departments` are structured for global read access by all authenticated users, meaning `list` queries on these collections are inherently secure and efficient. For the "Organization-Wide Achievement Feed," a collection group query on `achievements` is secured because each `Achievement` document includes its `userId`, allowing rules to grant read access to all authenticated users for all achievements, ensuring no private data is exposed inadvertently.

**Structural Segregation:**
Documents with different security requirements are placed in distinct collections. For instance, `user_profiles` handles personal data, while `news_posts` and `events` are dedicated to organization-wide, administrator-managed content. This simplification of security posture per collection makes rules clearer and less prone to errors.

**Access Modeling:**
*   **Private Data:** `UserProfile` data is stored under `/user_profiles/{userId}`, establishing path-based ownership where `userId` matches `request.auth.uid`.
*   **Hierarchical User Data:** For 1:N relationships like User to Achievement or User to Wish, the hierarchical path is continued (`/user_profiles/{userId}/achievements`, `/user_profiles/{recipientId}/received_wishes`), reinforcing ownership and specific recipient access.
*   **Global/Admin Data:** `Departments`, `NewsPost`, and `Event` entities reside in top-level collections (`/departments`, `/news_posts`, `/events`) that are broadly readable and admin-writeable.

This holistic design ensures that security rules are simple, efficient, and easy to debug, minimizing potential vulnerabilities and supporting the application's scalability.
